name: Email Abuse Intelligence — Campaign Taxonomy
model: Email Abuse Intelligence Reference Model v1.0

classes.name: Bulk advertising
classes.definition: Unsolicited promotion of products or services at scale.

classes.name: Affiliate / lead generation
classes.definition: Traffic and leads resold to third parties for commission.

classes.name: Scam & fraud
classes.definition: Deception aimed at direct financial loss.

classes.name: Malware delivery
classes.definition: Mail used as a carrier for a malicious payload.

classes.name: Credential phishing
classes.definition: Harvesting logins via deceptive destinations.

classes.name: List building / reputation
classes.definition: Probing and warming to enable later campaigns.

classes.name: Snowshoe
classes.definition: Volume spread thin across many IPs and domains to stay under thresholds.

classes.name: Botnet-origin
classes.definition: Sent from large numbers of compromised hosts.

classes.name: Compromised account
classes.definition: Abuse of a legitimate, hijacked mailbox or tenant.

classes.name: ESP / bulk-provider abuse
classes.definition: Legitimate sending platforms used for abusive lists.

classes.name: Hijacked infrastructure
classes.definition: Dormant subdomains, dangling DNS or routing abused to send.

classes.name: Direct-MTA
classes.definition: Self-hosted mail servers sending directly to recipients.

classes.name: Exact-domain spoofing
classes.definition: Forging a real domain that lacks enforced authentication.

classes.name: Lookalike / cousin domain
classes.definition: Homoglyph or typo variants of a trusted domain.

classes.name: Newly-registered domain
classes.definition: Freshly registered domains with no reputation history.

classes.name: Compromised legitimate domain
classes.definition: Real, reputable domains taken over to send.

classes.name: Unauthenticated sender
classes.definition: No SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment asserted at all.

classes.name: Content obfuscation
classes.definition: Hiding intent from filters via encoding or imagery.

classes.name: Redirect / cloaking chains
classes.definition: Multi-hop and conditional redirection to a payload.

classes.name: Content polymorphism
classes.definition: Per-recipient variation to defeat signatures.

classes.name: Send-and-burn
classes.definition: Short-lived infrastructure abandoned after a burst.

classes.name: Reputation warming
classes.definition: Gradual ramp-up to build sender trust before abuse.

classes.name: Mass / consumer
classes.definition: Untargeted, high-volume lures cast at the general public.

classes.name: Spear phishing
classes.definition: Tailored to a specific individual using researched detail.

classes.name: Whaling
classes.definition: Aimed at executives and other high-value decision-makers.

classes.name: Clone phishing
classes.definition: A copy of a genuine prior message with the link or attachment swapped.

classes.name: Vendor / supply-chain
classes.definition: Impersonating a trusted supplier or partner relationship.

classes.name: Account security
classes.definition: “Verify” or “secure” an account said to be at risk.

classes.name: Payment / invoice
classes.definition: Fake bills, receipts or payment-change requests.

classes.name: Delivery / shipping
classes.definition: Parcel, customs or delivery-failure notices.

classes.name: HR / payroll
classes.definition: Benefits, payslip or policy pretexts aimed at staff.

classes.name: IT / helpdesk
classes.definition: Password resets, mailbox quota or support pretexts.

classes.name: Document share
classes.definition: A shared file or signature request on a cloud service.

classes.name: Embedded link
classes.definition: A URL leading to an attacker-controlled destination.

classes.name: Attachment
classes.definition: A file that carries or assembles the lure or payload.

classes.name: QR code (quishing)
classes.definition: A link delivered as an image to pivot to a personal device.

classes.name: Reply-chain hijack
classes.definition: Insertion into a real, existing conversation thread.

classes.name: Malvertising / redirect
classes.definition: Arriving via advertising or a redirect network.

classes.name: Cloned login page
classes.definition: A fake sign-in page that records entered credentials.

classes.name: Adversary-in-the-middle
classes.definition: A live proxy that relays to the real site to steal the session.

classes.name: OAuth consent
classes.definition: Tricking a user into granting application permissions.

classes.name: OTP interception
classes.definition: Prompting the victim to hand over a one-time code in real time.

classes.name: Data-entry form
classes.definition: A multi-field form harvesting credentials and personal data.

classes.name: Executive (CEO fraud)
classes.definition: Posing as a senior leader to pressure a payment.

classes.name: Vendor / supplier (VEC)
classes.definition: Posing as a trusted supplier, often from a compromised supplier mailbox.

classes.name: Internal finance
classes.definition: Posing as a colleague in finance, payroll or accounts payable.

classes.name: Legal / authority
classes.definition: Posing as counsel, an auditor or a regulator to compel action.

classes.name: Trusted customer / partner
classes.definition: Posing as a known counterparty in an ongoing relationship.

classes.name: Wire transfer
classes.definition: A direct instruction to send funds.

classes.name: Invoice redirection
classes.definition: Altering the payee or account on a real payment.

classes.name: Payroll diversion
classes.definition: Redirecting an employee's salary deposit.

classes.name: Gift-card scam
classes.definition: Requesting prepaid cards as an untraceable payout.

classes.name: Data / payroll-record theft
classes.definition: Extracting tax or payroll data for later fraud.

classes.name: Lookalike domain
classes.definition: A cousin domain of the company or vendor.

classes.name: Compromised internal account
classes.definition: A genuine internal mailbox taken over via phishing.

classes.name: Compromised vendor account
classes.definition: A supplier's real mailbox used against its customers.

classes.name: Display-name only
classes.definition: No infrastructure — just a forged friendly name.

classes.name: Reply-chain hijack
classes.definition: Insertion into a real, ongoing payment conversation.

classes.name: Authority
classes.definition: Weight of a senior or official identity.

classes.name: Urgency
classes.definition: A deadline that discourages verification.

classes.name: Confidentiality
classes.definition: “Keep this quiet” to block a second opinion.

classes.name: Routine mimicry
classes.definition: Blending into normal, expected process.

classes.name: Established trust
classes.definition: Exploiting a real, existing relationship.

classes.name: Loader / downloader
classes.definition: A small first-stage that fetches and runs further malware.

classes.name: Info-stealer
classes.definition: Harvests credentials, cookies, wallets and files.

classes.name: Ransomware
classes.definition: Encrypts or exfiltrates data for extortion.

classes.name: Remote-access trojan
classes.definition: Grants an operator interactive control of the host.

classes.name: Banking trojan
classes.definition: Targets financial credentials and sessions.

classes.name: Botnet / worm
classes.definition: Enlists the host into a network for reuse or spread.

classes.name: Malicious attachment
classes.definition: An executable or script carried directly in the message.

classes.name: Macro document
classes.definition: An office document whose macros fetch or run a payload.

classes.name: Link to payload
classes.definition: A URL leading to a hosted download.

classes.name: HTML smuggling
classes.definition: A payload assembled in the browser from an HTML file.

classes.name: Archive container
classes.definition: A password-protected or nested archive hiding the payload.

classes.name: Cloud-hosted payload
classes.definition: The file staged on a reputable cloud or file service.

classes.name: Macro enable
classes.definition: The user is coaxed into enabling document macros.

classes.name: Script host
classes.definition: A shortcut or script (LNK, JS, HTA, VBS) invokes an interpreter.

classes.name: Document exploit
classes.definition: A vulnerability in the viewer runs code automatically.

classes.name: User run
classes.definition: The user is persuaded to double-click and run the file.

classes.name: Living-off-the-land
classes.definition: Trusted system binaries are abused to execute the payload.

classes.name: Password-gated archive
classes.definition: A password in the email keeps scanners out of the archive.

classes.name: Sandbox evasion
classes.definition: Execution is withheld from automated analysis environments.

classes.name: Packing / obfuscation
classes.definition: Code is compressed or scrambled to defeat signatures.

classes.name: Multi-stage
classes.definition: Capability is split across stages fetched only when needed.

classes.name: Abused trust / signing
classes.definition: Reputable services or code signatures lend false legitimacy.

classes.name: Advance-fee
classes.definition: A promised windfall unlocked only after upfront payments.

classes.name: Romance
classes.definition: A fabricated relationship leveraged for money over time.

classes.name: Investment / crypto
classes.definition: Fake high-return schemes and fraudulent trading platforms.

classes.name: Lottery / prize
classes.definition: A win or inheritance the recipient never entered.

classes.name: Employment
classes.definition: Fake job or task offers used to extract money or launder funds.

classes.name: Marketplace / goods
classes.definition: Fraud around fake purchases, rentals or sales.

classes.name: Windfall
classes.definition: The promise of unexpected money or reward.

classes.name: Affection
classes.definition: Emotional attachment and companionship.

classes.name: Greed
classes.definition: The lure of outsized, easy returns.

classes.name: Fear
classes.definition: Threats, blackmail or claimed legal jeopardy.

classes.name: Opportunity
classes.definition: A too-good job, deal or investment window.

classes.name: Authority
classes.definition: The weight of a claimed official or institution.

classes.name: Upfront fee
classes.definition: Fees, taxes or charges demanded before the promised payout.

classes.name: Bank / card details
classes.definition: Direct capture of payment credentials.

classes.name: Cryptocurrency
classes.definition: Irreversible transfers to attacker wallets.

classes.name: Gift cards
classes.definition: Prepaid card codes as an untraceable payout.

classes.name: Money-mule recruitment
classes.definition: The victim is turned into a launderer of others' funds.

classes.name: Personal-data harvest
classes.definition: Identity data collected for later fraud.

classes.name: One-shot blast
classes.definition: A single mass attempt with no follow-up.

classes.name: Long con
classes.definition: Patient grooming over days, weeks or months.

classes.name: Reload
classes.definition: Re-targeting a prior victim for a second loss.

classes.name: Escalation
classes.definition: Rising fees justified by ever-new obstacles.

classes.name: Exact domain
classes.definition: The real domain is forged in the visible From.

classes.name: Lookalike / cousin
classes.definition: A homoglyph or typo variant of the real domain.

classes.name: Display name
classes.definition: Only the friendly name is forged; the address is not.

classes.name: Subdomain / dangling
classes.definition: A dormant subdomain or dangling record is abused.

classes.name: Reply-to mismatch
classes.definition: A different reply-to quietly redirects the conversation.

classes.name: Homoglyph / IDN
classes.definition: Internationalised characters imitate familiar letters.

classes.name: No SPF
classes.definition: No sender-policy record to check against.

classes.name: Weak / no DKIM
classes.definition: No verifiable signature on the message.

classes.name: DMARC none
classes.definition: A policy that reports but never blocks.

classes.name: Misaligned auth
classes.definition: SPF or DKIM passes for a different domain than the From.

classes.name: Forwarder gaps
classes.definition: Legitimate forwarding breaks authentication and is exploited.

classes.name: Header-from
classes.definition: The visible From header is spoofed.

classes.name: Envelope-from
classes.definition: The hidden return-path is spoofed.

classes.name: Brand mimicry
classes.definition: Logos, layout and tone impersonate a brand.

classes.name: Trust-domain abuse
classes.definition: A genuinely trusted service or domain is misused.

classes.name: New-domain impersonation
classes.definition: A fresh domain poses as an established identity.

classes.name: Brand
classes.definition: A company or product identity.

classes.name: Executive / person
classes.definition: A named individual.

classes.name: Vendor / partner
classes.definition: A supplier or business relationship.

classes.name: Government / authority
classes.definition: A public body or regulator.

classes.name: Internal colleague
classes.definition: A coworker within the same organisation.
